channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Where the word 'Olympics' comes from and what it means to someone who competed there
item.description: It's a word that evokes national pride and rare talent, and one that has been around for thousands of years.

item.title: 8 creative ways to build your village, according to our listeners
item.description: NPR listeners share how they've made relationships with their neighbors and community. Many of them, through parties, potlucks and coffees, say they'v...

item.title: State public-option health plans expand but can't fill gaps left by federal changes
item.description: Nevada recently became the third state to offer one of the plans on the ACA marketplaces. They're intended to be a cheaper insurance option but so far...

item.title: Surprise four-legged Olympic competitor wows cross-country fans
item.description: Nazgul sprints on course at cross-country ski race, crossing finish line in unsanctioned quest for glory

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to face jury in landmark social media addiction trial
item.description: The case is seen as a test of social media's legal responsibility for platform design features that plaintiffs' lawyers say exacerbated mental health ...

item.title: Lindsey Vonn's Olympic crash rekindles concern over ski bindings design
item.description: Lindsey Vonn's devastating injury when her skis didn't come off has put a spotlight on bindings, which hold boots to skis and are some of the oldest t...

item.title: UK police force assessing Stansted Airport private flights over Epstein ties
item.description: Essex Police says it is assessing information in relation to private flights into and out of the airport.

item.title: Rescuers search for nine missing skiers after California avalanche
item.description: Rescuers are racing to the scene, which has seen about 30 inches (76cm) of snowfall in the last 24 hours.

item.title: See the Olympics schedule for the 2026 Milano Cortina Games
item.description: This is what you need to know about when the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics start and end, along with key games and events on the schedule.

item.title: Peru's Congress ousts president after four months in office
item.description: JosÃ© JerÃ­ is the third consecutive president to be removed and the country's seventh since 2016.

item.title: Climber on trial for leaving girlfriend to die on Austria's highest mountain
item.description: Kerstin G's boyfriend is accused of leaving her unprotected and exhausted close to the summit during a blizzard.

item.title: Rescuers push through winter storm to 6 survivors of a California avalanche. 9 others are missing
item.description: Crews have pushed through mountainous wilderness in California near Lake Tahoe during a snowstorm to rescue six backcountry skiers who survived an ava...

item.title: 2/17: CBS Evening News
item.description: Blinding dust storm leads to deadly pileup on Colorado highway; The "crying horse" toy taking over China's internet.

item.title: In a first, shark seen on camera in Antarctica waters, surprising researchers
item.description: A large shark was caught on camera for the first time in Antarctica's waters, surprising researchers. "There's a general rule of thumb that you don't ...

item.title: US says 'meaningful progress' made as Ukraine talks enter second day
item.description: The US special envoy struck an optimistic note after the first day of talks in Geneva, but hopes for a breakthrough remain low.

item.title: Epstein survivor tells CBS News how she was trafficked, and assaulted
item.description: Juliette Bryant says not long after meeting Jeffrey Epstein, he assaulted her, and she realized, "this is not a modeling opportunity, I've been kidnap...

item.title: Shooter killed ex-wife and a son in Rhode Island ice rink attack, police say
Minnesotaâs Legislature braces for a federal immigration fight as the enforcement surge winds down
